reasoning: The question requires a newly starting protest in India (not active at the time of the question's launch in mid-April 2025) to reach 10,000+ participants and be reported on the Carnegie Global Protest Tracker by July 1, 2025. While India frequently sees mass protests, there are major hurdles for a YES resolution. First, the large ongoing Waqf Act protests are explicitly disqualified by the 'active at launch' fine print. Second, any new protest would have to start and escalate within a short 2.5-month window that includes the extreme summer heat of May and June, which historically suppresses large outdoor gatherings. Third and most importantly, the Carnegie Global Protest Tracker is a manually curated think-tank database that appears to update periodically (often with annual or semi-annual reviews, as evidenced by their December 2023 and 2024 publications), rather than in real-time like ACLED. Therefore, even if a new mass protest occurs in May or June, it is highly unlikely to be logged on the tracker by the July 1 deadline. I estimate an 11% probability.
